name: Bbqed Chile-Marinated Pork Spareribs
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, combine the pork spareribs with enough water to cover. Bring to a boil and simmer until the ribs are tender, about 50 minutes. Drain the ribs well and pat them dry.
2. While the ribs are simmering, puree the dried New Mexican chiles, water, ketchup, garlic, vinegar, brown sugar, salt, tequila, vegetable oil, cumin, and allspice in a blender until smooth.
3. Generously coat the ribs with some of the chili sauce, reserving the remaining sauce in a small bowl covered with plastic wrap for at least 8 hours or overnight.
4. Let the ribs stand at room temperature for 1 hour before grilling them on an oiled rack set about 5-6 inches over the heat source for 6 minutes on each side.
5. Simmer the reserved chili sauce in a small saucepan for 3 minutes and serve with the ribs.
